{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2c83fa51e0074081b90bb189","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\n4\t\n5\t/// Result of a claim operation\n6\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n7\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n8\t    pub bead_id: String,\n9\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n10\t}\n11\t\n12\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n13\t///\n14\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n15\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n16\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n17\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n18\t/// 4. Insert an event\n19\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n20\t/// 6. Commit\n21\t///\n22\t/// # Arguments\n23\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n24\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n25\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n26\t/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n27\t///\n28\t/// # Returns\n29\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n30\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n31\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n32\tpub fn claim(\n33\t    tx: &Transaction,\n34\t    worker: &str,\n35\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n36\t    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n37\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n38\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n39\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n40\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n41\t        \"UPDATE issues\n42\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n43\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n44\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n45\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n46\t    )?;\n47\t\n48\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n49\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n50\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n51\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n52\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n53\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n54\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n55\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n56\t                i.priority\n57\t         FROM issues i\n58\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n59\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n60\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n61\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n62\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n63\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n64\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n65\t         GROUP BY i.id\n66\t         ORDER BY\n67\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n68\t             critical_float ASC,\n69\t             i.priority ASC,\n70\t             i.created_at ASC\n71\t         LIMIT 1\",\n72\t    )?;\n73\t\n74\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n75\t\n76\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n77\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n78\t\n79\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n80\t        tx.execute(\n81\t            \"UPDATE issues\n82\t             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n83\t             WHERE id = ?\",\n84\t            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n85\t        )?;\n86\t\n87\t        // Step 4: Insert event\n88\t        tx.execute(\n89\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n90\t             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n91\t            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n92\t        )?;\n93\t\n94\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n95\t        tx.execute(\n96\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n97\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n98\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n99\t        )?;\n100\t\n101\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n102\t            bead_id,\n103\t            reclaimed,\n104\t        }))\n105\t    } else {\n106\t        Ok(None)\n107\t    }\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t#[cfg(test)]\n111\tmod tests {\n112\t    use super::*;\n113\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n114\t    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n115\t\n116\t    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n117\t        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n118\t        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n119\t        (temp_file, storage)\n120\t    }\n121\t\n122\t    #[test]\n123\t    fn test_claim_basic() {\n124\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n125\t\n126\t        // Create an open bead\n127\t        let issue = claim_result = result.unwrap();\n137\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n138\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n139\t\n140\t        // Verify the bead is now idx_labels_label ON labels(label);\n119\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_issue ON labels(issue_id);\n120\t\n121\t    -- Comments\n122\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments (\n123\t CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n128\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n129\t    );\n130\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id);\n131\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_created_at ON comments(created_at);\n132\t\n133\t    -- Events (Audit)\n134\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (\n135\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n136\t        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n137\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n138\t        actor TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n139\t        old_value TEXT,\n140\t        new_value    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);\n146\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);\n147\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_created_at ON events(created_at);\n148\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n149\t\n150\t    -- Config (Runtime)\n151\t    -- NOTE: Avoid PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE constraints here because the current\n152\t    -- storage engine does not reliably maintain unique autoindexes.\n153\t    -- Application code enforces key replacement via DELETE + INSERT.\n154\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS config (\n155\t        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n156\t        value TEXT NOT NULL\n157\t    );\n158\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_config_key ON NULL,\n164\t        value TEXT NOT NULL\n165\t    );\n166\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metadata_key ON metadata(key);\n167\t\n168\t    -- Dirty Issues (for export)\n169\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT -- `blocked_by` stores a JSON array of blocking issue IDs.\n187\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n188\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n189\t        blocked_by TEXT NOT );\n201\t\n202\t    -- Critical Path Cache (for float-based ordering in claim operations)\n203\t    -- Stores the critical path float value for each bead.\n204\t    -- Lower float = more critical (closer to root of critical path).\n205\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS critical_path_cache (\n206\t        bead_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n207\t        float REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 999999,\n208\t ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n227\t\n228\t    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n229\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n230\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n231\t        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n234\t        anomaly_type TEXT,\n235\t        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n236\t        outcome TEXT,\n237\t        evidence TEXT,\n238\t        metadata TEXT,\n239\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n240\t anomaly_audit(event_type);\n245\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n246\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n247\t\";\n248\t\n249\t/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n250\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n251\t///\n252\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n253\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n254\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n255\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n256\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n257\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n258\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n259\t///\n260\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n261\t/// statement terminators.\n262\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n263\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n264\t    let len = bytes.len();\n265\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n266\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n267\t    let mut i = 0;\n268\t\n269\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n270\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n271\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n272\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n273\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n274\t\n275\t    while i < len {\n276\t        let b = bytes[i];\n277\t\n278\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n279\t        if in_line_comment {\n280\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n281\t                in_line_comment = false;\n282\t            }\n283\t            i += 1;\n284\t            continue;\n285\t        }\n286\t\n287\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n288\t        if in_block_comment {\n289\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n290\t                in_block_comment = false;\n291\t                i += 2;\n292\t            } else {\n293\t                i += 1;\n294\t            }\n295\t            continue;\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n299\t        if in_single_quote {\n300\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n301\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n302\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n303\t                    i += 2;\n304\t                } else {\n305\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n306\t                    i += 1;\n307\t                }\n308\t            } else {\n309\t                i += 1;\n310\t            }\n311\t            continue;\n312\t        }\n313\t\n314\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n315\t        if in_double_quote {\n316\t            if b == b'\"' {\n317\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n318\t                    i += 2;\n319\t                } else {\n320\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n321\t                    i += 1;\n322\t                }\n323\t            } else {\n324\t                i += 1;\n325\t            }\n326\t            continue;\n327\t        }\n328\t\n329\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n330\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n331\t            in_single_quote = true;\n332\t            i += 1;\n333\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n334\t            in_double_quote = true;\n335\t            i += 1;\n336\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n337\t            in_line_comment = true;\n338\t            i += 2;\n339\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n340\t            in_block_comment = true;\n341\t            i += 2;\n342\t        } else if b == b';' {\n343\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n344\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n345\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n346\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n347\t            }\n348\t            start = i + 1;\n349\t            i += 1;\n350\t        } else {\n351\t            i += 1;\n352\t        }\n353\t    }\n354\t\n355\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n356\t    if start < len {\n357\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n358\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n359\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    stmts\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n367\tfn execute_batch(conn: &rusqlite::Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n368\t    for stmt in split_sql_statements(sql) {\n369\t        conn.execute(stmt, [])?;\n370\t    }\n371\t    Ok(())\n372\t}\n373\t\n374\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n375\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n376\t    Ok(())\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\tpub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n380\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL\", [])?;\n381\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\", [])?;\n382\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000\", [])?;\n383\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA cache_size = -8000\", [])?;\n384\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL\", [])?;\n385\t    Ok(())\n386\t}\n387\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_8e48ee5be232482194f6626f","session_id":"b764733b-eb0f-4ca4-94d8-9e96cfe3e616","uuid":"8a6e8e88-cb76-4972-99f8-019a4f4a7530","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:57:31.617Z"}

continue;\n70\t                }\n71\t                Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n72\t            }\n73\t        }\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    pub fn get_issue(&self, id: &str) -> &issue.notes,\n231\t                    &issue.status.to_string(),\n232\t                    &issue.priority,\n233\t                    &issue.issue_type.to_string(),\n234\t let mut updates = Vec::new();\n307\t            let mut params: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n308\t\n309\t            if let Some(ref title) = changes.title {\n310\t                updates.push(\"title = ?\");\n311\t                params.push(title.clone());\n312\t            }\n313\t            if let Some(ref description) = changes.description {\n314\t                updates.push(\"description = ?\");\n315\t                params.push(description.clone());\n316\t            }\n317\t            if let Some(ref status) = changes.status {\n318\t                updates.push(\"status = ?\");\n319\t                params.push(status.to_string());\n320\t            }\n321\t            if let Some(priority) = changes.priority {\n322\t                updates.push(\"priority = ?\");\n323\t                params.push(priority.to_string());\n324\t            }\n325\t            if let Some(ref issue_type) = changes.issue_type {\n326\t                updates.push(\"issue_type = ?\");\n327\t                params.push(issue_type.to_string());\n328\t            }\n329\t            if let Some(ref assignee) = changes.assignee {\n330\t                updates.push(\"assignee = ?\");\n331\t                params.push(assignee.clone());\n332\t            }\n333\t\n334\t            if !updates.is_empty() {\n335\t                updates.push(\"updated_at = ?\");\n336\t              let mut all_params = params.clone();\n341\t                all_params.push(id.to_string());\n342\t\n343\t                let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n344\t                    all_params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n345\t                tx.execute(&query, param_refs.as_slice())?;\n346\t            }\n347\t\n348\t            Ok(())\n349\t        })\n350\t    }\n351\t\n352\t    pub fn close_issue(&self, id: &str, reason: &str, actor: &str) -> Result<()> {\n353\t        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n354\t            let now = }\n622\t\n623\t    pub fn search_issues(\n624\t        &self,\n625\t        query: Option<&str>,\n626\t        status: &[Status],\n627\t        issue_type: &[IssueType],\n628\t        assignee: Option<&str>,\n629\t        labels: &[String],\n630\t        priority_min: Option<i32>,\n631\t        priority_max: Option<i32>,\n632\t        limit: usize,\n633\t    ) -> Result<Vec<Issue>> {\n634\t        let mut sql = String::from(\n635\t            \"SELECT DISTINCT i.id, i.content_hash, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,\n636\t                    i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.owner, i.estimated_minutes,\n637\t                    i.created_at, i.created_by, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.close_reason,\n638\t                    i.closed_by_session, i.due_at, i.defer_until, i.external_ref, i.source_system,\n639\t                    i.source_repo, i.deleted_at, i.deleted_by, i.delete_reason, i.original_type,\n640\t                    i.compaction_level, i.compacted_at, i.compacted_at_commit, i.original_size,\n641\t                    i.sender, i.ephemeral, i.pinned, i.is_template\n642\t             FROM issues i\n643\t             LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n644\t             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n645\t        );\n646\t\n647\t        let mut params = Vec::new();\n648\t        let mut param_idx = 1;\n649\t\n650\t        if let Some(q) = query {\n651\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND (i.title LIKE ?{} OR i.description LIKE ?{})\", param_idx, param_idx + 1));\n652\t            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n653\t            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n654\t            param_idx += 2;\n655\t        }\n656\t\n657\t        for s in status {\n658\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n659\t            params.push(s.to_string());\n660\t            param_idx += 1;\n661\t        }\n662\t\n663\t        for t in issue_type {\n664\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n665\t            params.push(t.to_string());\n666\t            param_idx += 1;\n667\t        }\n668\t\n669\t        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n670\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n671\t            params.push(a.to_string());\n672\t            param_idx += 1;\n673\t        }\n674\t\n675\t        if !labels.is_empty() {\n676\t            let label_conditions: Vec<String> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, _)| {\n677\t                format!(\"l.label = ?{}\", param_idx + i)\n678\t            }).collect();\n679\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n680\t            for label in labels {\n681\t                params.push(label.clone());\n682\t                param_idx += 1;\n683\t            }\n684\t        }\n685\t\n686\t        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n687\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n688\t            params.push(min.to_string());\n689\t            param_idx += 1;\n690\t        }\n691\t\n692\t        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n693\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n694\t            params.push(max.to_string());\n695\t            param_idx += 1;\n696\t        }\n697\t\n698\t        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n699\t\n700\t        if limit > 0 {\n701\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n702\t        }\n703\t\n704\t        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n705\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n706\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n707\t\n708\t        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n709\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n710\t            issues.push(self.row_to_issue(row)?);\n711\t        }\n712\t        Ok(issues)\n713\t    }\n714\t\n715\t    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n716\t        let total: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE usize,\n751\t}\n752\t\n753\tfn is_busy_error(e: &rusqlite::Error) -> bool {\n754\t    matches!(\n755\t        e,\n756\t        rusqlite::Error::SqliteFailure(\n757\t            rusqlite::ffi::Error { {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_acf077961dad4531ad4ef4be","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse rand::Rng;\n2\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n3\tuse num_bigint::BigUint;\n4\tuse num_traits::{One, Zero};\n5\tuse anyhow::Result;\n6\t\n7\tconst BASE36_CHARS: &[u8] = b\"0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz\";\n8\t\n9\t/// Calculate optimal hash length using the birthday problem formula.\n10\t///\n11\t/// For a given expected number of items N and hash length L with base B:\n12\t///   P(collision) ≈ 1 - exp(-N² / (2 * B^L))\n13\t///\n14\t/// Solving for L given target collision probability p:\n15\t///   L ≈ log_B(N² / (2 * -ln(1-p)))\n16\t///\n17\t/// We use p = 0.05 (5% collision probability) to balance br compatibility\n18\t/// with mathematical soundness, and clamp L to [3, 8].\n19\tpub fn optimal_hash_length(existing_count: usize) -> usize {\n20\t    if existing_count == 0 {\n21\t        return 3;\n22\t    }\n23\t\n24\t    // Target: 5% collision probability (balance between br compatibility and safety)\n25\t    const TARGET_COLLISION_PROB: f64 = 0.05;\n26\t    let neg_ln_one_minus_p = -((1.0 - TARGET_COLLISION_PROB).ln());\n27\t\n28\t    // B = 36 (base36), solve for L\n29\t    // L = log_36(N² / (2 * -ln(1-p)))\n30\t    let n = existing_count as f64;\n31\t    let numerator = n * n;\n32\t    let denominator = 2.0 * neg_ln_one_minus_p;\n33\t    let base = 36.0_f64;\n34\t\n35\t    let length = (numerator / denominator).log(base).ceil() as usize;\n36\t\n37\t    // Clamp to [3, 8] range\n38\t    length.clamp(3, 8)\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\tpub fn base36_encode(data: &[u8]) -> String {\n42\t    let mut result = String::new();\n43\t    let num = BigUint::from_bytes_be(data);\n44\t    let base = BigUint::from(36u32);\n45\t\n46\t    if num.is_zero() {\n47\t        return \"0\".to_string();\n48\t    }\n49\t\n50\t    let mut n = num;\n51\t    while n > BigUint::zero() {\n52\t        let remainder = &n % &base;\n53\t        let digit = remainder.to_u32_digits()[0] as usize;\n54\t        result.insert(0, BASE36_CHARS[digit] as char);\n55\t        n /= &base;\n56\t    }\n57\t\n58\t    result\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\tpub fn generate_id(prefix: &str, existing_count: usize) -> String {\n62\t    let len = optimal_hash_length(existing_count);\n63\t    let random_bytes: [u8; 16] = }\n79\t    let hash_part = parts[1..].join(\"\");\n80\t    hash_part.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric())\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\t#[cfg(test)]\n84\tmod tests {\n85\t    use super::*;\n86\t\n87\t }\n106\t\n107\t    #[test]\n108\t    fn test_is_valid_bead_id() {\n109\t        assert!(is_valid_bead_id(\"bf-abc123\"));\n110\t        assert!(is_valid_bead_id(\"bd-a1b2c3\"));\n111\t }\n133\t\n134\t        Ok(results)\n135\t    })\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\tfn execute_create(\n139\t    tx: &Transaction,\n140\t    title: &str,\n141\t    type_: &str,\n142\t    priority: i32,\n143\t     &[parent],\n233\t        |row| row.get(0),\n234\t    )?;\n235\t\n236\t    let child_exists: bool = tx.query_row(\n237\t        \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM issues WHERE id = ?1)\",\n238\t        &[child],\n239\t        |row| row.get(0),\n240\t    )?;\n241\t\n242\t    if !parent_exists {\n243\t        return Err(anyhow!(\"Parent bead not found: {}\", parent));\n244\t    }\n245\t    if )?;\n262\t\n263\t    Ok(())\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\tfn execute_close(tx: &Transaction, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n267\t    // Verify bead exists\n268\t    let exists: bool = tx.query_row(\n269\t        \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM issues WHERE id = ?1)\",\n270\t        &[id],\n271\t        |row| row.get(0),\n272\t    )?;\n273\t\n274\t    if !exists {\n275\t        return    )?;\n290\t\n291\t    Ok(())\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\tpub fn parse_stdin() -> Result<Vec<BatchOp>> {\n295\t    let mut input = String::new();\n296\t    std::io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input)?;\n297\t\n298\t    // Try JSON first\n299\t    if let Ok(ops) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BatchOp>>(&input) {\n300\t        return Ok(ops);\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    // Fall back to CLI-style parsing (one op per line)\n304\t    let mut ops = Vec::new();\n305\t    for line in input.lines() {\n306\t        let line = line.trim();\n307\t        if line.is_empty() || line.starts_with('#') {\n308\t            continue;\n309\t        }\n310\t\n311\t        // Simple parsing for: create --title \"X\" --type Y\n312\t        if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"create \") {\n313\t            ops.push(parse_create(rest)?);\n314\t        } else if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"dep add-blocker \") {\n315\t            ops.push(parse_dep_add(rest)?);\n316\t        } else if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"close \") {\n317\t            ops.push(parse_close(rest)?);\n318\t        } else {\n319\t            return Err(anyhow!(\"Unknown operation: {}\", line));\n320\t        }\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    Ok(ops)\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\tfn parse_create(input: &str) -> Result<BatchOp> {\n327\t    let mut title = None;\n328\t    let mut type_ = \"task\".to_string();\n329\t    let mut priority = 2;\n330\t    let mut description = None;\n331\t\n332\t    let parts = shell_words::split(input)?;\n333\t    let mut i = 0;\n334\t    while i < parts.len() {\n335\t        match parts[i].as_str() {\n336\t            \"--title\" => {\n337\t                i += 1;\n338\t                if i < parts.len() {\n339\t {\n345\t                    type_ = parts[i].clone();\n346\t                }\n347\t            }\n348\t            \"--priority\" => {\n349\t                i += 1;\n350\t                if i < parts.len() {\n351\t                    priority = parts[i].parse().unwrap_or(2);\n352\t                }\n353\t            }\n354\t            \"--description\" => {\n355\t                i += 1;\n356\t                if i < parts.len() {\n357\t                    description = Some(parts[i].clone());\n358\t                }\n359\t            }\n360\t            _ => {\n361\t description,\n376\t        assignee: None,\n377\t        labels: Vec::new(),\n378\t    })\n379\t}\n380\t\n381\tfn parse_dep_add(input: &str) -> Result<BatchOp> {\n382\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = input.split_whitespace().collect();\n383\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n384\t        return Err(anyhow!(\"dep add-blocker requires parent and child IDs\"));\n385\t    }\n386\t    Ok(BatchOp::DepAddBlocker {\n387\t        parent: parts[0].to_string(),\n388\t        child: parts[1].to_string(),\n389\t    })\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\tfn parse_close(input: &str) -> Result<BatchOp> {\n393\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = input.split_whitespace().collect();\n394\t    let id = parts.first().ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Missing ID for close operation\"))?;\n395\t    let reason = if parts.len() > 1 {\n396\t        parts[1..].join(\" \")\n397\t    } else {\n398\t        \"Completed\".to_string()\n399\t    };\n400\t    Ok(BatchOp::Close {\n401\t        id: id.to_string(),\n402\t })\n404\t}\n405\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_8e48ee5be232482194f6626f","session_id":"b764733b-eb0f-4ca4-94d8-9e96cfe3e616","uuid":"8aafd47d-1792-48a3-818e-447b1d00e0cc","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:57:38.461Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_51a7a6afd9bd4af7a11afd02","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# bead-forge\n2\t\n3\tDrop-in replacement for [beads_rust](https://github.com/dicklesworthstone/beads_rust) (`br`) with built-in server mode for concurrent multi-worker coordination.\n4\t\n5\t## Problem\n6\t\n7\t`br` uses SQLite for bead storage. SQLite serializes writes, causing contention when multiple NEEDLE workers claim beads from the same workspace simultaneously. With 11+ workers, the thundering herd problem wastes cycles on claim retries and database busy errors.\n8\t\n9\t## Solution\n10\t\n11\t`bead-forge` (`bf`) provides two modes:\n12\t\n13\t- **Standalone**: Identical to `br` — SQLite + JSONL, fully backward compatible\n14\t- **Server**: Built-in coordination server with atomic claiming — no races, no retries, zero contention at any worker count\n15\t\n16\t## Status\n17\t\n18\tResearch phase. See [docs/research/](docs/research/) for analysis.\n19\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_8e48ee5be232482194f6626f","session_id":"b764733b-eb0f-4ca4-94d8-9e96cfe3e616","uuid":"fd2c94a9-1a28-4fe0-8aa0-f29713e74d29","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:57:45.086Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2a0ff1c41a254700b57bf827","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# bead-forge (`bf`)\n2\t\n3\t![bead-forge hero](hero.png)\n4\t\n5\t**bead-forge** is a Rust CLI that replaces `br` (beads_rust) as the bead management tool for AI-supervised coding workflows. It is a drop-in replacement for `br` — every command, flag, and output format is identical — with one critical improvement: **concurrent claiming is correct**.\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## The Problem It Solves\n10\t\n11\t`br` has a race condition at its core. Claiming a bead requires two separate operations:\n12\t\n13\t```\n14\tbr ready --json          # read candidates (snapshot)\n15\t  ↓  (race window)\n16\tbr update <id> --status in_progress --assignee <worker>\n17\t  ↓  (race window)\n18\tbr show <id>             # verify the claim stuck\n19\t```\n20\t\n21\tWith 20 NEEDLE workers running simultaneously, multiple workers read the same candidate list, all select the same top bead, and all issue `br update`. SQLite last-writer-wins — 19 workers hold phantom claims on beads they don't actually own.\n22\t\n23\t`bf` eliminates this with a single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` SQLite transaction:\n24\t\n25\t```\n26\tbf claim --assignee worker-7 --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --harness needle --json\n27\t```\n28\t\n29\tThe entire read-score-update sequence runs inside one write lock. No two workers can observe the same candidate state.\n30\t\n31\t---\n32\t\n33\t## Architecture\n34\t\n35\t```\n36\tSQLite (live store)                    issues.jsonl (git artifact)\n37\t    │                                        │\n38\t    │  all reads and writes                  │  exported by bf sync --flush\n39\t    │                                        │  imported by bf sync --import\n40\t    ▼                                        ▼\n41\t  beads.db                            .beads/issues.jsonl\n42\t  (gitignored)                        (committed to git)\n43\t```\n44\t\n45\t**SQLite is the live database.** JSONL is a derived snapshot committed to git for backup, cross-machine sharing, and recovery. `beads.db` is rebuilt at any time with `bf doctor --repair`.\n46\t\n47\t**No daemon. No server. No flock.** Each `bf` invocation is self-contained: open DB, acquire write lock, execute, release, exit. SQLite WAL mode enables concurrent reads during writes.\n48\t\n49\t---\n50\t\n51\t## Key Features\n52\t\n53\t### Atomic Concurrent Claiming\n54\t\n55\t```\n56\tWorker 1: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT + score → UPDATE winner → COMMIT\n57\tWorker 2: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → (blocked until Worker 1 commits) → SELECT → ...\n58\t...\n59\tWorker 20: BEGIN IMMEDIATE → SELECT → NONE (queue empty) → COMMIT\n60\t```\n61\t\n62\tThe entire claim pipeline — stale reclamation, candidate scoring, winner selection, status update, event recording — runs in a single SQLite write transaction. Guaranteed correct under any concurrency level.\n63\t\n64\t### Critical Path Scoring\n65\t\n66\t`bf` computes the **float** of each bead: how many hops it can slip before delaying its epic. Beads on the critical path (`float == 0`) receive a 1000-point bonus in the claim scorer, ensuring the fleet works on the most impactful beads automatically — without human priority curation.\n67\t\n68\t```\n69\t$ bf critical-path bf-epic-123\n70\t\n71\t  float=0  [bf-a3f8] Implement auth token refresh        in_progress\n72\t  float=0  [bf-b2c1] Fix concurrent session state bug    open\n73\t  float=2  [bf-d5e3] Update API documentation            open\n74\t```\n75\t\n76\t### Velocity-Aware Scoring\n77\t\n78\tWorkers declare their composition when claiming:\n79\t\n80\t```bash\n81\tbf claim --assignee worker-7 \\\n82\t         --model claude-sonnet-4-6 \\\n83\t         --harness needle \\\n84\t         --harness-version 0.5.2 \\\n85\t         --json\n86\t```\n87\t\n88\t`bf` tracks close times per `(model, harness, issue_type)` in `velocity_stats`. The claim scorer weights candidates by `impact / expected_duration` — the fleet maximizes throughput per unit time, not just raw priority. After 10+ completions per cohort, routing becomes measurably smarter.\n89\t\n90\t```\n91\t$ bf velocity\n92\t\n93\tModel                  Harness   Type     Samples  p50    p90\n94\tclaude-opus-4-7        needle    task     142      8m     22m\n95\tclaude-sonnet-4-6      needle    task     87       18m    45m\n96\tclaude-haiku-4-5       needle    task     23       35m    2h10m\n97\t```\n98\t\n99\t### Atomic Batch Operations\n100\t\n101\tReplaces NEEDLE's crash-unsafe create+dep chains:\n102\t\n103\t```bash\n104\tbf batch --json '[\n105\t  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Implement login handler\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n106\t  {\"op\": \"create\", \"title\": \"Add session tests\", \"type\": \"task\"},\n107\t  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"bf-a3f8.1\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n108\t  {\"op\": \"dep_add_blocker\", \"parent\": \"bf-a3f8.2\", \"child\": \"bf-a3f8\"},\n109\t  {\"op\": \"close\", \"id\": \"bf-a3f8\", \"reason\": \"Split into children\"}\n110\t]'\n111\t```\n112\t\n113\tAll operations execute in one `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` transaction. A crash mid-batch leaves zero partial state — SQLite rolls back automatically.\n114\t\n115\t### Extensible Annotations\n116\t\n117\tArbitrary key-value metadata on any bead, transparent to `br`:\n118\t\n119\t```bash\n120\tbf annotate bf-a3f8 needle_attempt=3 needle_session=abc123 review_status=needs_review\n121\tbf list --annotation needle_attempt=3\n122\t```\n123\t\n124\t### Operation History\n125\t\n126\t```bash\n127\tbf log bf-a3f8\n128\t\n129\t2026-04-29 10:00  CREATED     by: human                  \"Implement auth flow\"\n130\t2026-04-29 14:30  CLAIMED     by: worker-3 (sonnet-4-6)  open → in_progress\n131\t2026-04-29 15:45  COMMENT     by: worker-3               \"Found edge case\"\n132\t2026-04-29 16:00  CLOSED      by: worker-3               \"Completed\"\n133\t```\n134\t\n135\t---\n136\t\n137\t## Commands\n138\t\n139\t```\n140\tbf create        --title \"...\" --type <type> --priority <N> [--label ...]\n141\tbf list          [--status <s>] [--type <t>] [--assignee <a>] [--format json|text|toon]\n142\tbf show          <id>\n143\tbf update        <id> [--status <s>] [--assignee <a>] ...]\n144\tbf close         <id> [--reason \"...\"]\n145\tbf claim         --assignee <id> [--model <m>] [--harness <h>] [--harness-version <v>]\n146\t                 [--workspace <path>] [--any]\n147\tbf critical-path <epic-id>\n148\tbf batch         [--file ops.json] [--json '[...]']\n149\tbf annotate      <id> key=value [--remove key]\n150\tbf log           [<id>] [--since <date>] [--actor <a>] [--diff]\n151\tbf rotate        [--age-days N]\n152\tbf velocity      [--model <m>] [--harness <h>]\n153\tbf dep           add-blocker <blocker> <blockee>\n154\tbf dep           tree <id>\n155\tbf label         <id> add <label> [<label> ...]\n156\tbf sync          [--flush-only] [--import-only]\n157\tbf doctor        [--repair] [--check] [--reclaim-stale [--ttl <duration>]]\n158\tbf init          [--prefix <p>]\n159\tbf stats\n160\tbf search        <query>\n161\t```\n162\t\n163\tAll `br` commands work identically. `bf` is a strict superset.\n164\t\n165\t---\n166\t\n167\t## NEEDLE Integration\n168\t\n169\tReplace the five non-atomic `br` chains in `bead_store/mod.rs`:\n170\t\n171\t| Old (racy) | New (atomic) |\n172\t|-----------|--------------|\n173\t| `br ready` → `br update` → `br show` (3 calls, 2 race windows) | `bf claim` (1 call, 1 transaction) |\n174\t| `br create` + `br dep add` (orphan if crash between) | `bf batch` (crash-safe, all-or-nothing) |\n175\t| `br show` just to get `.labels` field | `bf labels <id>` (direct `SELECT` on labels table) |\n176\t| N × `br label add` loops (N processes) | `bf label <id> add l1 l2 l3` (one transaction) |\n177\t\n178\tBackward compatibility — install `br` as a symlink:\n179\t\n180\t```bash\n181\tln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n182\t```\n183\t\n184\t---\n185\t\n186\t## Compatibility\n187\t\n188\t`bf` reads and writes the same SQLite schema and JSONL format as `br`. A workspace used by `bf` can be read by `br` and vice versa. Verified by three test suites (see plan §Compatibility Verification):\n189\t\n190\t1. **JSONL round-trip**: `br list --format json` == `bf list --format json` on the same workspace\n191\t2. **SQLite compat**: `bf` writes, `br` reads, `br doctor` passes\n192\t3. **Claim race**: 20 concurrent `bf claim` processes, 10 beads — exactly 10 claims succeed, no bead claimed twice\n193\t\n194\t---\n195\t\n196\t## Reliability\n197\t\n198\t- **rusqlite** (standard SQLite C library) — not FrankenSQLite, which had known corruption under concurrent writes\n199\t- **WAL mode** — concurrent reads never block writes; writers queue, not corrupt\n200\t- **`BEGIN IMMEDIATE`** — acquires write lock before any reads; eliminates TOCTOU\n201\t- **`SQLITE_BUSY` retry** — exponential backoff up to 5 retries; converts contention spikes to short waits\n202\t- **JSONL always authoritative** — `bf doctor --repair` rebuilds `beads.db` from `issues.jsonl` at any time\n203\t\n204\t---\n205\t\n206\t## Implementation\n207\t\n208\tSee [`docs/plan/plan.md`](plan/plan.md) for the complete implementation plan including:\n209\t\n210\t- Exact SQLite schema (all 13 tables + indexes)\n211\t- `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` retry wrapper implementation\n212\t- Critical path CTE algorithm\n213\t- Velocity stats schema and claim scoring formula\n214\t- Compatibility verification test suites\n215\t- NEEDLE integration specifics (file, line numbers, before/after)\n216\t\n217\t---\n218\t\n219\t## Build & Deploy\n220\t\n221\tBuilt via Argo Workflows on `iad-ci`. WorkflowTemplate: `bead-forge-build` in `jedarden/declarative-config`.\n222\t\n223\t```bash\n224\t# Install\n225\tcurl -L https://github.com/jedarden/bead-forge/releases/latest/download/bf-linux-x86_64 \\\n226\t  -o ~/.local/bin/bf && chmod +x ~/.local/bin/bf\n227\t\n228\t# Drop-in replace br\n229\tln -sf ~/.local/bin/bf ~/.local/bin/br\n230\t\n231\t# Verify\n232\tbf --version\n233\tbf init --prefix bf\n234\tbf create \"first bead\" --type task\n235\t```\n236\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_8e48ee5be232482194f6626f","session_id":"b764733b-eb0f-4ca4-94d8-9e96cfe3e616","uuid":"ab061c6d-9d7a-48d3-9eb1-fcd45bbd71cf","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:57:45.087Z"}

anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Transaction,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","numLines":187,"startLine":1,"totalLines":187}}}

=> {\n            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n        }\n        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format } => {\n            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Show { id, format } => cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n        }\n        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Ready { limit, format } => cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format),\n        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, format } => {\n            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n    }\n}\n\nfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n\n    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n    if !config_path.exists() {\n        let config = format!(\n            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\ndefault_priority: 2\ndefault_type: task\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n\"#,\n            prefix\n        );\n        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n    }\n\n    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n    }\n\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_create(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    title: String,\n    type_: String,\n    priority: i32,\n    description: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    labels: Vec<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n\n    let now = Utc::now();\n    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n    issue.description = description;\n    issue.assignee = assignee;\n    issue.labels = labels;\n\n    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n\n    println!(\"{}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_list(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    type_: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    filter.assignee = assignee;\n    filter.priority = priority;\n    filter.limit = limit;\n\n    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issue)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&issue)?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n            println!(\"Status: {}\", issue.status);\n            println!(\"Priority: {}\", issue.priority);\n            println!(\"Type: {}\", issue.issue_type);\n            if let Some(desc) = &issue.description {\n                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n            }\n            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n            }\n            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_update(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    id: &str,\n    title: Option<String>,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut changes = IssueChanges {\n        title,\n        status: status.map(|s| Status::from_str(&s).ok()).flatten(),\n        priority,\n        assignee,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Updated bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_close(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.close_issue(id, reason, \"cli\")?;\n    println!(\"Closed bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let changes = IssueChanges {\n        status: Some(Status::Open),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Reopened bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_delete(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    })?;\n\n    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    filter.status = Some(Status::Open);\n    filter.limit = Some(limit);\n\n    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issue)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_claim(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    assignee: &str,\n    _model: Option<String>,\n    _harness: Option<String>,\n    _harness_version: Option<String>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n    let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now())\n    })?;\n\n    match result {\n        Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed }) => {\n            match format {\n                \"json\" => {\n                    let output = serde_json::json!({\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                        \"assignee\": assignee\n                    });\n                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None => {\n            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    if import_only {\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n    } else if flush_only {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    } else {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    if repair {\n        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n        let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Repaired database: imported {} beads from JSONL\", result.imported);\n    } else {\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n        println!(\"Database is healthy: {} beads\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = if let Some(s) = status {\n        let filter = IssueFilter {\n            status: Some(Status::from_str(&s).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n    } else {\n        storage.count_issues()?\n    };\n\n    println!(\"{}\", count);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n    } else if stdin {\n        parse_stdin()?\n    } else {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n    };\n\n    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n\n    // Print results\n    for result in results {\n        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n            } else {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n            }\n        } else {\n            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n","numLines":615,"startLine":1,"totalLines":615}}}

database\",\n26\t    \"file is not a database\",\n27\t];\n28\t\n29\t/// Known error strings that indicate br sync conflicts.\n30\tconst SYNC_CONFLICT_MARKERS: &[&str] = &[\"SYNC_CONFLICT\", \"JSONL is newer\", \"sync conflict\"];\n31\t\n32\t/// Check if an error message indicates SQLite database corruption.\n33\t///\n34\t/// Returns `true` if the message contains any known corruption lower.contains(marker))\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t/// Check if an error message indicates a br sync conflict.\n43\t///\n44\t/// Returns `true` if the message contains any known sync conflict marker.\n45\tpub /// Exclude beads that have any of these labels.\n81\t    pub exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// ─── RepairReport Vec<String>,\n90\t    pub fixed: Vec<String>,\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\t// ─── BeadStore trait ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n94\t\n95\t/// Abstract interface to the bead backend.\n96\t#[async_trait]\n97\tpub trait BeadStore: Send + Sync {\n98\t    /// List all beads with no incomplete blockers (ready to work on).\n99\t    ///\n100\t    /// Returns an empty `Vec` when /// List ALL beads in the workspace (no readiness/filter checks).\n105\t    ///\n106\t    /// Used by Knot strand for three-state verification — a DIFFERENT code\n107\t    /// path from `ready()` to avoid v1's false-positive bug.\n108\t    async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>>;\n109\t\n110\t    /// Fetch a single bead by ID.\n111\t    async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead>;\n112\t\n113\t    /// Attempt to atomically claim a bead (set status=in_progress, assignee=actor).\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// Returns a `ClaimResult` describing the outcome:\n116\t    /// - `Claimed(bead)` — success, returns the full bead.\n117\t    /// - `RaceLost { claimed_by }` — another worker got there first.\n118\t    /// - `NotClaimable { reason }` — bead not in a claimable state.\n119\t    async fn claim(&self, id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult>;\n120\t\n121\t    /// Atomically find and claim the next available bead (server-selected).\n122\t    ///\n123\t    /// This is the preferred method for multi-worker scenarios as it eliminates\n124\t    /// race conditions: the server selects the bead and assigns it in a single\n125\t    /// BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction. Two workers calling this simultaneously will\n126\t    /// always receive distinct beads.\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// Returns a `ClaimResult` describing the outcome:\n129\t    /// - `Claimed(bead)` — success, returns the full bead.\n130\t    /// - `NotClaimable { reason }` — no beads available to claim.\n131\t    async fn claim_auto(&self, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult>;\n132\t\n133\t    /// Release a claimed bead back to open (e.g., after agent failure).\n134\t    async fn release(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()>;\n135\t\n136\t    /// Flush local bead changes to JSONL before release.\n137\t    ///\n138\t    /// Runs `br sync --flush-only` to ensure any local writes are persisted\n139\t    /// to JSONL before attempting to release a bead.    /// Create a new bead and return its ID.\n159\t    async fn create_bead(&self, title: &str, body: &str, labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId>;\n160\t\n161\t    /// Add a dependency link: `blocker_id` fix them.\n177\t    async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport>;\n178\t\n179\t    /// Full database rebuild: remove SQLite DB and reimport from JSONL.\n180\t    ///\n181\t    /// 1. rm install beads_rust\")?;\n224\t        Ok(BrCliBeadStore { br_path, workspace })\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    /// Default timeout for br subprocess calls (30 seconds).\n228\t    const DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 30;\n229\t\n230\t    /// Run a `br` subcommand in the workspace directory and return stdout.\n231\t    ///\n232\t    /// Returns `Err` if the process fails to spawn, exits non-zero (unless\n233\t    /// the caller handles specific codes), or stdout is not valid UTF-8.\n234\t    async fn run_br(&self, args: &[&str]) -> Result<String> {\n235\t        self.run_br_in(&self.workspace, args, Self::DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS)\n236\t            .await\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    /// Run a `br` subcommand with a custom timeout.\n240\t    ///\n241\t    /// Use this for calls that may take longer (e.g., sync operations).\n242\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n243\t    async fn run_br_with_timeout(&self, args: &[&str], timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<String> {\n244\t        self.run_br_in(&self.workspace, args, timeout_secs).await\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    async fn run_br_in(&self, dir: &Path, args: &[&str], timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<String> {\n248\t        let timeout_duration = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);\n249\t\n250\t        // kill_on_drop ensures the process is killed if the wait_with_output\n251\t        // future is dropped (e.g., on timeout), preventing orphaned br processes.\n252\t        let mut cmd = tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path);\n253\t        cmd.args(args)\n254\t            .current_dir(dir)\n255\t            .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n256\t            .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n257\t            .kill_on_drop(true);\n258\t        let child = cmd\n259\t            .spawn()\n260\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to spawn br subprocess: {args:?}\"))?;\n261\t\n262\t        // Wait for output with timeout. On timeout, kill_on_drop fires automatically.\n263\t        let output = match tokio::time::timeout(timeout_duration, child.wait_with_output()).await {\n264\t            Ok(Ok(output)) => output,\n265\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n266\t                return Err(e).context(format!(\"br subprocess failed: {args:?}\"));\n267\t            }\n268\t            Err(_) => {\n269\t                tracing::error!(\n270\t                    args = ?args,\n271\t                    timeout_secs,\n272\t                    \"br subprocess timed out, killing process\"\n273\t                );\n274\t                bail!(\"br subprocess timed out after {timeout_secs}s: {args:?}\");\n275\t            }\n276\t        };\n277\t\n278\t        let stdout = String::from_utf8(output.stdout).context(\"br stdout was not valid UTF-8\")?;\n279\t        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();\n280\t\n281\t        if !output.status.success() {\n282\t            let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\n283\t\n284\t            // FrankenSQLite crash recovery: if br was killed by a signal\n285\t            // (code() returns None) but stdout shows the operation completed\n286\t            // and stderr is empty, treat as success. This commonly happens\n287\t            // when br's SQLite layer crashes during post-commit cleanup while\n288\t            // the mutation was already persisted to the append-only JSONL file.\n289\t            if output.status.code().is_none() && stderr.is_empty() && !stdout.is_empty() {\n290\t                tracing::warn!(\n291\t                    args = ?args,\n292\t                    stdout = %stdout.trim(),\n293\t                    \"br was killed by signal but stdout indicates success — \\\n294\t                     treating as successful (FrankenSQLite crash recovery)\"\n295\t                );\n296\t                return Ok(stdout);\n297\t            }\n298\t\n299\t            // Auto-recover from SYNC_CONFLICT: run `br sync` then retry once.\n300\t            if is_sync_conflict(&stderr) {\n301\t                tracing::warn!(\n302\t                    args = ?args,\n303\t                    \"br hit SYNC_CONFLICT, running br sync and retrying\"\n304\t                );\n305\t\n306\t                let sync_timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(60);\n307\t                let mut sync_cmd = tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path);\n308\t                sync_cmd.args([\"sync\"])\n309\t                    .current_dir(dir)\n310\t                    .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n311\t                    .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n312\t                    .kill_on_drop(true);\n313\t                let sync_child = sync_cmd\n314\t                    .spawn()\n315\t                    .context(\"failed to spawn br sync during SYNC_CONFLICT recovery\")?;\n316\t\n317\t                let sync_output = match tokio::time::timeout(\n318\t                    sync_timeout,\n319\t                    sync_child.wait_with_output(),\n320\t                )\n321\t                .await\n322\t                {\n323\t                    Ok(Ok(output)) => output,\n324\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n325\t                        return Err(e).context(\"br sync failed during SYNC_CONFLICT recovery\");\n326\t                    }\n327\t                    Err(_) => {\n328\t                        tracing::error!(\"br sync timed out after 60s during SYNC_CONFLICT recovery, killing process\");\n329\t                        bail!(\"br sync timed out after 60s during SYNC_CONFLICT recovery\");\n330\t                    }\n331\t                };\n332\t\n333\t                if !sync_output.status.success() {\n334\t                    let sync_stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&sync_output.stderr);\n335\t                    tracing::warn!(stderr = %sync_stderr, \"br sync failed, retrying original command anyway\");\n336\t                }\n337\t\n338\t                // Retry the original command once with timeout.\n339\t                let mut retry_cmd = tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path);\n340\t                retry_cmd.args(args)\n341\t                    .current_dir(dir)\n342\t                    .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n343\t                    .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())\n344\t                    .kill_on_drop(true);\n345\t                let retry_child = retry_cmd\n346\t                    .spawn()\n347\t                    .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to spawn br retry with args: {args:?}\"))?;\n348\t\n349\t                let retry =\n350\t                    match tokio::time::timeout(timeout_duration, retry_child.wait_with_output())\n351\t                        .await\n352\t                    {\n353\t                        Ok(Ok(output)) => output,\n354\t                        Ok(Err(e)) => {\n355\t                            return Err(e).context(format!(\"br retry failed: {args:?}\"));\n356\t                        }\n357\t                        Err(_) => {\n358\t                            tracing::error!(\n359\t                                args = ?args,\n360\t                                timeout_secs,\n361\t                                \"br retry timed out, killing process\"\n362\t                            );\n363\t                            bail!(\"br retry subprocess timed out after {timeout_secs}s: {args:?}\");\n364\t                        }\n365\t                    };\n366\t\n367\t                let retry_stdout = String::from_utf8(retry.stdout)\n368\t                    .context(\"br retry stdout was not valid UTF-8\")?;\n369\t                let retry_stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&retry.stderr).into_owned();\n370\t\n371\t                if !retry.status.success() {\n372\t                    let retry_code = retry.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\n373\t                    return Err(anyhow::Error::new(SyncRecoveryError {\n374\t                        reason: format!(\n375\t                            \"exit code {retry_code} after br sync retry\\n\\\n376\t                             stderr: {retry_stderr}\\nstdout: {retry_stdout}\"\n377\t                        ),\n378\t                    }));\n379\t                }\n380\t\n381\t                return Ok(retry_stdout);\n382\t            }\n383\t\n384\t            bail!(\"br {args:?} exited with code {code}\\nstderr: {stderr}\\nstdout: {stdout}\");\n385\t        }\n386\t\n387\t        Ok(stdout)\n388\t    }\n389\t\n390\t    /// Run br and return both exit code and stdout (for claim race detection).\n391\t    ///\n392\t    /// Auto-recovers from SYNC_CONFLICT (exit code 6): runs `br sync` then\n393\t    /// retries the original command once.\n394\t    async fn run_br_with_status(&self, args: &[&str]) -> Result<(i32, String)> {\n395\t        let timeout_duration = std::time::Duration::from_secs(Self::DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS);\n396\t\n397\t        // kill_on_drop ensures the process is killed if the wait_with_output\n398\t        // future is dropped (e.g., on timeout), preventing orphaned br processes.\n399\t        let mut cmd = tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path);\n400\t        cmd.args(args)\n401\t            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n402\t            .kill_on_drop(true);\n403\t        let child = cmd\n404\t            .spawn()\n405\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to spawn br subprocess: {args:?}\"))?;\n406\t\n407\t        let output = match tokio::time::timeout(timeout_duration, child.wait_with_output()).await {\n408\t            Ok(Ok(output)) => output,\n409\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n410\t                return Err(e).context(format!(\"br subprocess failed: {args:?}\"));\n411\t            }\n412\t            Err(_) => {\n413\t                tracing::error!(\n414\t                    args = ?args,\n415\t                    timeout_secs = Self::DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS,\n416\t                    \"br subprocess timed out, killing process\"\n417\t                );\n418\t                bail!(\n419\t                    \"br subprocess timed out after {timeout_secs}s: {args:?}\",\n420\t                    timeout_secs = Self::DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS\n421\t                );\n422\t            }\n423\t        };\n424\t\n425\t        let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\n426\t        let stdout = String::from_utf8(output.stdout).context(\"br stdout was not valid UTF-8\")?;\n427\t        let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);\n428\t\n429\t        // Auto-recover from SYNC_CONFLICT: run `br sync` then retry once.\n430\t        if code != 0 && is_sync_conflict(&stderr) {\n431\t            tracing::warn!(\n432\t                args = ?args,\n433\t                \"br hit SYNC_CONFLICT (run_br_with_status), running br sync and retrying\"\n434\t            );\n435\t            let sync_timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(60);\n436\t            let _ = tokio::time::timeout(\n437\t                sync_timeout,\n438\t                tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path)\n439\t                    .args([\"sync\"])\n440\t                    .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n441\t                    .output(),\n442\t            )\n443\t            .await;\n444\t\n445\t            let retry = tokio::time::timeout(\n446\t                timeout_duration,\n447\t                tokio::process::Command::new(&self.br_path)\n448\t                    .args(args)\n449\t                    .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n450\t                    .output(),\n451\t            )\n452\t            .await\n453\t            .with_context(|| {\n454\t                format!(\n455\t                    \"br retry subprocess timed out after {timeout_secs}s: {args:?}\",\n456\t                    timeout_secs = Self::DEFAULT_BR_TIMEOUT_SECS\n457\t                )\n458\t            })?\n459\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to spawn br retry with args: {args:?}\"))?;\n460\t\n461\t            let retry_code = retry.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);\n462\t            let retry_stdout =\n463\t                String::from_utf8(retry.stdout).context(\"br retry stdout was not valid UTF-8\")?;\n464\t            return Ok((retry_code, retry_stdout));\n465\t        }\n466\t\n467\t        Ok((code, stdout))\n468\t    }\n469\t\n470\t    /// Parse a JSON array of beads from br output.\n471\t    fn parse_beads(json: &str, context: &str) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n472\t        if json.trim().is_empty() {\n473\t            return Ok(vec![]);\n474\t        }\n475\t        serde_json::from_str::<Vec<Bead>>(json)\n476\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"JSON parse error from {context}:\\n{json}\"))\n477\t    }\n478\t\n479\t    /// Parse a single bead from a JSON array (first element).\n480\t    fn parse_single_bead(json: &str, context: &str) -> Result<Bead> {\n481\t        let beads = Self::parse_beads(json, context)?;\n482\t        beads\n483\t            .into_iter()\n484\t            .next()\n485\t            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"{context} returned empty array\"))\n486\t    }\n487\t}\n488\t\n489\t#[async_trait]\n490\timpl BeadStore for BrCliBeadStore {\n491\t    async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n492\t        let stdout = self\n493\t            .run_br(&[\"list\", \"--json\", \"--limit\", \"0\"])\n494\t            .await\n495\t            .context(\"br list --json failed\")?;\n496\t        Self::parse_beads(&stdout, \"br list --json\")\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n500\t        let mut args = vec![\"ready\", \"--json\", \"--limit\", \"0\"];\n501\t\n502\t        // Build filter args — stored so they live long enough for the slice.\n503\t        let assignee_arg;\n504\t        if let Some(ref assignee) = filters.assignee {\n505\t            args.push(\"--assignee\");\n506\t            assignee_arg = assignee.clone();\n507\t            args.push(&assignee_arg);\n508\t        }\n509\t\n510\t        let stdout = self.run_br(&args).await.context(\"br ready failed\")?;\n511\t        let mut beads = Self::parse_beads(&stdout, \"br ready --json\")?;\n512\t\n513\t        // Apply label exclusion filter (br CLI doesn't support this natively).\n514\t        if !filters.exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n515\t            beads.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n516\t        }\n517\t\n518\t        Ok(beads)\n519\t    }\n520\t\n521\t    async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n522\t        let id_str = id.as_ref();\n523\t        let stdout = self\n524\t            .run_br(&[\"show\", id_str, \"--json\"])\n525\t            .await\n526\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"br show {id_str} failed\"))?;\n527\t        Self::parse_single_bead(&stdout, &format!(\"br show {id_str} --json\"))\n528\t    }\n529\t\n530\t    async fn claim(&self, id: &BeadId, actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n531\t        let id_str = id.as_ref();\n532\t        // Attempt claim by setting status=in_progress and assignee.\n533\t        let (code, _stdout) = self\n534\t            .run_br_with_status(&[\n535\t                \"update\",\n536\t                id_str,\n537\t                \"--status\",\n538\t                \"in_progress\",\n539\t                \"--assignee\",\n540\t                actor,\n541\t            ])\n542\t            .await\n543\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"br update {id_str} (claim) failed to spawn\"))?;\n544\t\n545\t        match code {\n546\t            0 => {\n547\t                // Verify we actually won by reading back the bead.\n548\t                let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n549\t                if bead.assignee.as_deref() == Some(actor) {\n550\t                    Ok(ClaimResult::Claimed(bead))\n551\t                } else {\n552\t                    let claimed_by = bead\n553\t                        .assignee\n554\t                        .clone()\n555\t                        .unwrap_or_else(|| \"(unknown)\".to_string());\n556\t                    Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost { claimed_by })\n557\t                }\n558\t            }\n559\t            4 => {\n560\t                // br exit code 4 signals a conflict / optimistic lock failure.\n561\t                let bead = self.show(id).await.ok();\n562\t                let claimed_by = bead\n563\t                    .and_then(|b| b.assignee)\n564\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"(unknown)\".to_string());\n565\t                Ok(ClaimResult::RaceLost { claimed_by })\n566\t            }\n567\t            _ => Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n568\t                reason: format!(\"br update exited with code {code}\"),\n569\t            }),\n570\t        }\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    async fn release(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n574\t        let id_str = id.as_ref();\n575\t        self.run_br(&[\"update\", id_str, \"--status\", \"open\", \"--assignee\", \"\"])\n576\t            .await\n577\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"br release {id_str} failed\"))?;\n578\t        Ok(())\n579\t    }\n580\t\n581\t    async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n582\t        self.run_br(&[\"sync\", \"--flush-only\"])\n583\t            .await\n584\t            .context(\"br sync --flush-only failed\")?;\n585\t        Ok(())\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    async fn reopen(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n589\t        let id_str = id.as_ref();\n590\t    }\n618\t\n619\t    async fn create_bead(&self, title: &str, body: &str, labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n620\t        let mut args: Vec<String> = vec![\n621\t
